nativeRange.habitat: Lagoon and inshore reef areas; tends to inhabit coral or rock outcroppings situated in sandy areas at depths of 10–90 m

diet.notes: Has been documented accepting a variety of foods including frozen brine shrimp, mysis shrimp, dried seaweed, and high-quality flake or pellet foods. Marine algae-based foods are recommended as a regular part of the diet.

summary: A small, hardy damselfish with a distinctive white body and three bold black vertical bands. Generally considered beginner-friendly, though tend to be territorial and aggressive as adults, and are best kept with robust tankmates in an aquarium with ample rockwork.
careNotes: The Tuxedo Damselfish (trade name; current valid scientific name is Amblypomacentrus tricinctus, formerly Chrysiptera tricincta) is a hardy and generally easy-to-keep reef fish. It is commonly considered a good choice for beginner marine aquarists, though its aggressive tendencies as an adult should not be underestimated. Sources describe it as ranging from semi-aggressive to aggressive, and it has been known to harass smaller or more docile tankmates, particularly when defending territory in the rockwork. Providing an aquarium with plenty of hiding places and established territories tends to reduce conflict. Despite its assertiveness, it is generally considered reef-safe and has not been reported to harm corals or disturb aquascaping. Males tend to be slightly larger than females; sexing is otherwise difficult. The species is oviparous; males guard and aerate demersal eggs attached to the substrate. Juveniles may display somewhat subdued coloration compared to adults. Temperature cross-source note: retail sources suggest 22–26°C while FishBase wild-data indicates 24.3–28.9°C; the range here is averaged at 23–27°C.
breedingNotes: Has been documented as oviparous with distinct pair bonding during breeding. Males guard and aerate demersal eggs. Breeding in captivity has been reported but is considered relatively uncommon among hobbyists.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Direct species page found via search; the collection URL supplied in the manifest did not list this species directly.

additional.name: BlueZoo Aquatics
additional.notes: Confirmed: 2.5 in adult size, 30 gal minimum tank, aggressive temperament, reef safe, beginner care level. Salinity and temperature parameters not explicitly stated.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: FishBase redirects Chrysiptera tricincta to Amblypomacentrus tricinctus (Allen & Randall, 1974), confirming the current valid scientific name. Wild temperature range 24.3–28.9°C; adult size 6.0 cm TL; western Pacific distribution from Indonesia to Samoa.

additional.name: SaltwaterFish.com
additional.notes: Confirmed 30 gal minimum tank, 74–78°F temperature, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, reef safe, beginner level. Lifespan stated as 5–7 years. Adult size given as up to 4 inches (slightly higher than other sources).

additional.name: Quality Marine
additional.notes: Confirmed: ~3 in adult size, 30 gal minimum, 72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, dKH 8–12, territorial/aggressive temperament, reef safe, beginner level.

fish.breedingNotes: Oviparous; males guard and aerate demersal eggs. Captive breeding has been reported but is not common in the hobby.
